title: Annual CIP-002 Categorization Review Evidence Package

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-002-6 R4 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.4: Annual CIP-002 Categorization Review Evidence Package.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.4' running on index=itsm sourcetype=cip:review_ticket, index=cip_evidence sourcetype=cip:search_evidence, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=cip:review_ticket review_type="CIP-002_Categorization" earliest=-400d
| eval signed_epoch=strptime(signed_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval annual_due=relative_time(now(),"-1y@d")
| eval signed_ok=if(signed_epoch>=annual_due,1,0)
| append [
    search index=cip_evidence sourcetype=cip:search_evidence search_name="CIP002_inventory_recon" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(_time) as last_proof by search_name
  ]
| table review_type, owner, signed_at, signed_ok, last_proof
description: We timestamp inventory validation searches and signed review workflows so annual categorization review evidence is searchable and complete.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cip_evidence, itsm and sourcetypes cip:review_ticket, cip:review_ticket`, cip:search_evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Emit HEC when the responsible manager signs the annual review. (2) Schedule the inventory recon search to write `cip:search_evidence` events. (3) Alert if `signed_ok=0` approaching the compliance date. (4) PDF the dashboard for the audit binder. Indexes required: `index=cip_evidence`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip:review_ticket`, `sourcetype=cip:review_ticket``, `sourcetype=cip:search_evidence`, `sourcetype=cip:search_evidence``.
visualization: Single value (review current), Timeline (signatures vs proof runs), Table (owners)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for we timestamp inventory validation searches and signed review workflows so annual categorization review evidence is searchable and complete. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=cip:review_ticket`, `index=cip_evidence` `sourcetype=cip:search_evidence`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit HEC when the responsible manager signs the annual review. (2) Schedule the inventory recon search to write `cip:search_evidence` events. (3) Alert if `signed_ok=0` approaching the compliance date. (4) PDF the dashboard for the audit binder.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=cip:review_ticket review_type="CIP-002_Categorization" earliest=-400d
| eval signed_epoch=strptime(signed_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval annual_due=relative_time(now(),"-1y@d")
| eval signed_ok=if(signed_epoch>=annual_due,1,0)
| append [
    search index=cip_evidence sourcetype=cip:search_evidence search_name="CIP002_inventory_recon" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(_time) as last_proof by search_name
  ]
| table review_type, owner, signed_at, signed_ok, last_proof
```

Understanding this SPL

**Annual CIP-002 Categorization Review Evidence Package (CIP-002-6 R4)** — We timestamp inventory validation searches and signed review workflows so annual categorization review evidence is searchable and complete.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=cip:review_ticket`, `index=cip_evidence` `sourcetype=cip:search_evidence`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: cip:review_ticket. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=cip:review_ticket, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **signed_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **annual_due** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **signed_ok** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Appends rows from a subsearch with `append`.

Output fields: annual_due, last_proof, owner, review_type, signed_at, signed_epoch, signed_ok.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (review current), Timeline (signatures vs proof runs), Table (owners)
